questions.question_text: You intend to take the shortcut and continue straight ahead. How will you continue?

answers.1: I am particularly mindful of the traffic sign.
answers.2: I proceed with caution.
answers.3: I stop in order to turn around or reverse and find another route.
answers.4: I have a duty to give way to all traffic when I leave the area.

questions.question_text: You are driving at 40 km/h and are required to continue straight ahead. How will you continue?

answers.1: I continue at an unchanged speed of 40 km/h.
answers.2: I am particularly mindful of the pedestrian.
answers.3: I pull slightly to the left in the traffic lane.
answers.4: I move my foot over to the brake pedal.

questions.question_text: You have reduced your speed in order to turn right at the intersection. How will you continue?

answers.1: I turn right immediately.
answers.2: I stop at the stop line.
answers.3: I apply my brakes.
answers.4: In the first instance, I drive a little way into the intersection, stop at the bicycle path, and wait until the lane is clear before turning.

questions.question_text: You are driving at approximately 50 km/h and are required to continue straight ahead at the intersection. You have caught up with the braking red passenger car. How will you continue?

answers.1: I continue at an unchanged speed of 50 km/h.
answers.2: I overtake the red passenger car immediately.
answers.3: I am particularly mindful of the white passenger car waiting at the intersection.
answers.4: I wait before overtaking and remain behind the red passenger car for the time being.

questions.question_text: You are driving at 30 to 40 km/h and are required to continue straight ahead. There is no traffic behind you. How will you continue?

answers.1: I continue at an unchanged speed of 30 to 40 km/h.
answers.2: I warn the pedestrian with the horn.
answers.3: I apply my brakes.
answers.4: I am particularly mindful of pedestrians.

questions.question_text: You are driving at 20 to 30 km/h. How will you continue?

answers.1: I overtake the bicyclists immediately.
answers.2: I warn them with the horn.
answers.3: I wait until the bicyclists have passed the parked passenger car before overtaking them.
answers.4: I reduce my speed.

questions.question_text: You are driving at 30 km/h in a built-up area. What must you be particularly mindful of in this situation?

answers.1: I am particularly mindful of the traffic sign.
answers.2: I continue at 30 km/h after the traffic sign.
answers.3: After the traffic sign, I increase my speed and continue at a maximum of 50 km/h.
answers.4: There is a duty to give way to all traffic at the intersection.

questions.question_text: You are entering a built-up area and are required to continue straight ahead. How will you continue?

answers.1: I am particularly mindful of the traffic sign.
answers.2: After the traffic sign, I may drive at a maximum of 80 km/h.
answers.3: After the traffic sign, I may drive at a maximum of 50 km/h.
answers.4: I have a duty to give way to all traffic when I leave the area.

questions.question_text: You want to turn right as soon as possible. There is no traffic in your blind spots. How will you continue?

answers.1: I am particularly mindful of the traffic lane lines.
answers.2: I move into the traffic lane to my right.
answers.3: I wait before moving into the traffic lane on the right until the passenger car behind has passed me.
answers.4: I continue in my own traffic lane.

questions.question_text: You are driving at 50 km/h and are about to overtake the truck on your right. How will you continue?

answers.1: I increase my speed and overtake the truck.
answers.2: I reduce my speed and abandon the idea of overtaking the truck.
answers.3: I follow the rule on changing traffic lanes where my traffic lane ends.
answers.4: I follow the merge rule where my traffic lane ends.

questions.question_text: You are driving at 60 to 70 km/h and are required to continue straight ahead. How will you continue?

answers.1: I continue at an unchanged speed of 60 to 70 km/h.
answers.2: I reduce my speed.
answers.3: I count on the tractor holding back for me.
answers.4: I apply my brakes.

questions.question_text: You are driving at approximately 60 km/h and want to enter the motorway to the left. There is no traffic in your blind spot. How will you continue?

answers.1: I increase my speed.
answers.2: I activate the left indicator.
answers.3: I merge onto the motorway in front of the truck.
answers.4: I brake and wait before changing traffic lanes.

questions.question_text: You are driving at approximately 70 km/h. How will you continue?

answers.1: I continue at an unchanged speed of approximately 70 km/h.
answers.2: I reduce my speed.
answers.3: I am particularly mindful of the drivers in front.
answers.4: I am particularly mindful of the traffic signs.

questions.question_text: You are driving at 60 to 70 km/h. How will you continue?

answers.1: I am particularly mindful of the shape of the road.
answers.2: I am particularly mindful of a dangerous bend in the road.
answers.3: I reduce my speed and continue carefully further along the road.
answers.4: I increase my speed slightly.

questions.question_text: You are required to turn right here. There are no bicyclists behind you on the bicycle path. How will you continue?

answers.1: I turn right immediately.
answers.2: I flash my headlights or give a brief horn signal.
answers.3: I wait for the oncoming bicyclist to pass.
answers.4: I am particularly mindful that I am about to cross a two-lane bicycle path.

questions.question_text: The oncoming truck has just appeared. How will you continue?

answers.1: I continue further along the road.
answers.2: I pull over as far as possible to the right of the traffic lane.
answers.3: I increase my speed.
answers.4: I stop and wait for the oncoming traffic.

questions.question_text: You are driving at 80 km/h and are required to continue straight ahead. How will you continue?

answers.1: I reduce my speed.
answers.2: I am particularly mindful of the traffic lane lines.
answers.3: I am particularly mindful of the traffic further ahead.
answers.4: I stop at the stop line.

questions.question_text: You are driving at approximately 70 km/h. How will you continue?

answers.1: I continue at an unchanged speed of approximately 70 km/h.
answers.2: I reduce my speed.
answers.3: I am particularly mindful of riders.
answers.4: I am particularly mindful of wild animals.

questions.question_text: You want to turn right at this intersection. There are no bicyclists or pedestrians behind you on the bicycle path. How will you continue?

answers.1: I follow the bicyclist who is turning.
answers.2: I am particularly mindful of the traffic lights.
answers.3: I stop in front of the crosswalk on the side street.

questions.question_text: You want to turn right at this intersection. The traffic lane to the left is clear, and there are no bicyclists behind you. How will you continue?

answers.1: I am particularly mindful of the pedestrian.
answers.2: I stop in front of the crosswalk on the side street.
answers.3: I increase my speed.

questions.question_text: You want to turn right at the intersection. There is no traffic behind you. How will you continue?

answers.1: I hold back at the Give Way sign if there is crossing traffic.
answers.2: I turn right if the lane is clear of traffic.
answers.3: I have to turn left at this intersection.
answers.4: It is prohibited to turn left at the intersection.

questions.question_text: You want to turn left at this intersection. What must you be particularly mindful of?

answers.1: I move forward to the centre of the intersection.
answers.2: I am particularly mindful of oncoming traffic.
answers.3: I turn left immediately.
answers.4: I hold back for oncoming traffic.

questions.question_text: The driver has stepped out of the grey passenger car here. According to the legal provisions on stopping and parking, which of the following apply here?

answers.1: I am permitted to stop the passenger car here for up to 3 minutes.
answers.2: I am permitted to park the passenger car here.

questions.question_text: When is the driver responsible for ensuring that passengers are wearing seat belts?

answers.1: The driver is responsible for ensuring that all passengers under 15 years of age are correctly fastened with a seat belt.
answers.2: The driver is responsible only for passengers in the front seat.
answers.3: Without a seat belt, the risk of being thrown out of the passenger car in an accident increases.
answers.4: While driving, the airbag may be directly life-threatening if the seat belt is not used.

questions.question_text: What must you be particularly mindful of a little further ahead?

answers.1: I am particularly mindful of the shape of the road.
answers.2: I am particularly mindful of a dangerous bend in the road.
answers.3: I hold back for traffic from the side roads.
answers.4: I am particularly mindful that crossing traffic at the intersection further ahead has a duty to give way to all traffic.
